name: Velvet Chicken Under Snow
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Condensed milk or light cream
recipeInstructions:
1. The term "velvet" denotes a method of poaching chicken breast to turn it white and make the texture very soft and smooth. Care must be taken to use simmering liquid for just long enough to cook the chicken through. Boiling water or prolonged poaching will toughen the texture.
2. Velvet chicken: Heat 6 cups of water to boil. Reduce to simmer. Remove skin from chicken breast; cut breast into 1-inch chunks. Simmer in uncovered pan for 3-5 minutes, until meat is cooked through.
3. Sauce: In a wok or saucepan, heat chicken stock, sherry, ginger juice, sugar, and salt. When the sauce is very hot, slowly add condensed milk or cream. Stir to combine. Do not allow sauce to boil. Dribble in cornstarch paste to thicken. Enrich with sesame oil.
4. Rice stick: Heat oil in a wok for deep-frying until hot enough to puff up rice stick but not brown it. Fry in small batches; drain. Place rice stick on platter; cover with chicken.
